skill.name: nx-monorepo
skill.description: Nx monorepo management guidance for AI assistants. It covers project graph analysis, affected commands, generators, caching, Nx Cloud setup, and mixed TypeScript or Python workspaces.
skill.summary: Guides AI assistants through Nx workspace analysis, task orchestration, generators, caching, and monorepo workflows.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns, but the weak-crypto, hidden-file, reconnaissance, and path-traversal hits are false positives from Markdown examples. The real risk is that the skill instructs agents to run Nx, package-manager, GitHub CLI, and uv commands that can modify workspaces or contact services if used without approval.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Directed CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill tells agents to run Nx, pnpm, uv, git, and GitHub CLI commands. This is legitimate for an Nx workflow, but it can change files, install packages, run builds, create pull requests, or alter repository state if executed without user approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain direct command examples intended for agent use, and several commands can mutate the workspace or remote repository. The context is legitimate monorepo automation, so this is an operational risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: External Service and Package Manager Operations
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill includes commands for Nx Cloud connection, package plugin installation, GitHub pull request actions, and dependency setup. These actions can contact external services or install code, so users should approve them explicitly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands clearly invoke package managers and remote developer services. The files do not show credential theft or suspicious endpoints, so the confidence is high for network exposure but not for malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Static Hits
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The cited lines are Markdown metadata, prose, headings, or templates. No evidence found of weak hash algorithms, cryptographic APIs, or security-sensitive encryption logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found documentation text only. There are no code paths or examples that use MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak primitives.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Path Traversal Static Hits
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The flagged cd ../.. examples return from newly created app or library folders to the repository root. They are not user-controlled path traversal or file access logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The navigation appears inside setup instructions and uses fixed relative paths after creating known directories. No dynamic input or sensitive file target is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Hidden File and Reconnaissance Hits
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The hidden path is a CODEOWNERS example for /.claude/, and the cited reconnaissance line is review checklist prose. No evidence found of hidden file harvesting or system inventory collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden path is part of a repository ownership template, and the review checklist does not execute commands. The surrounding context is governance guidance, not data collection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Fenced Commands Intended for Agent Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains many shell command examples that an AI assistant may execute during normal use. This pattern is acceptable for a development skill, but it requires confirmation before commands that install dependencies, create branches, run generators, or contact remote services.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are real and operational, but they are transparent and relevant to Nx monorepo work. No destructive command, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection was found.

content.value_statement: Large Nx workspaces can be hard to inspect, test, and change safely. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured Nx workflows for graphs, affected tasks, generators, caching, and CI.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to inspect Nx project graphs and focused dependency views.
Guides affected build, test, and lint commands for changed projects.
Provides generator examples for Next.js, React, JavaScript, and Python workspace projects.
Documents local cache, Nx Cloud connection, and cache input configuration patterns.
Includes CI workflow examples for affected-only lint, build, and test tasks.
Adds monorepo workflow guidance for PR stacking, review, ownership, and RFC coordination.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include executable scripts or a custom Nx plugin.
Commands may modify files, install packages, or contact external services when executed.
Project names and paths are examples and must be adapted to the current workspace.
Nx MCP tools are referenced for documentation lookup but are not bundled with the skill.

use_cases.title: Analyze a Large Workspace
use_cases.description: Use project graph and affected commands to understand dependencies before changing shared libraries.

use_cases.title: Generate Workspace Projects
use_cases.description: Create Nx applications or libraries with consistent commands and verify them in the workspace graph.

use_cases.title: Improve Monorepo CI
use_cases.description: Apply affected-only pipelines, ownership rules, and review workflows to reduce CI time and coordination risk.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect the Workspace
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Nx workspace. Show me the project graph commands to run, the files you need to inspect, and the likely project boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Run Affected Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me identify projects affected since origin/main. Recommend build, test, and lint commands, and ask before running any command.

prompt_templates.title: Add a New Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through adding a new Nx project for this workspace. Include generator options, dry-run checks, and graph verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize CI and Ownership
prompt_templates.prompt: Review our Nx CI and monorepo ownership model. Propose affected-only tasks, cache improvements, CODEOWNERS changes, and rollout steps.

output_examples.input: What should I run before changing a shared UI library?
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends inspecting the focused project graph, listing affected projects from the main branch, then running affected lint, test, and build tasks before editing.

output_examples.input: How do I add a Python app to this Nx workspace?
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines directory creation, uv workspace setup, a project configuration, dependency sync, and Nx verification steps.

output_examples.input: Our CI runs every project on each pull request.
output_examples.output: The assistant suggests affected-only commands, cacheable target checks, Nx Cloud review, and ownership gates for risky cross-project changes.

best_practices: Use dry-run generation and graph checks before making large workspace changes.
Ask for approval before running commands that install packages, write files, or contact services.
Keep affected-task commands tied to an explicit base and head branch.
anti_patterns: Do not run package installation or Nx Cloud setup without user approval.
Do not reuse example project names without checking the real workspace structure.
Do not treat graph output as a substitute for code review on shared dependencies.

faq.question: Does this skill install Nx or plugins automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides commands and workflow guidance. The user should approve any installation command before execution.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require Nx MCP tools?
faq.answer: No. MCP tools are recommended for documentation lookup, while normal Nx operations use the Nx CLI.

faq.question: Can it handle Python projects in an Nx workspace?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes uv workspace guidance and manual project configuration examples for Python apps and libraries.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it includes commands that can modify repositories or contact services.

faq.question: What should users review before running commands?
faq.answer: Users should check package installation, generator output, branch operations, pull request actions, and external service connections.
